case_title: STANLEY WONG et al VS. DAVID WONG et al

docket_entries.description: MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-12-2003 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

OTHER NON EXEMPT COMPLAINTS, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF WONG, STANLEY WONG, MOON T. WONG, FUNG SUN AS TO DEFENDANT WONG, DAVID WONG, BILL THE WONG FAMILY BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION DOES 1-50, INCLUSIVE SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR MAY-14-2004 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  FEB-09-2004 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         APR-29-2004

LAW AND MOTION 301, EX PARTE HEARING RE: INJUNCTION THE COURT FINDS: THAT THE INTEGRITY OF THE 12/14/03 PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION ORDERED BY THE COURT ON 11/14/03 BY STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES WILL BE ENDANGERED IF ANY ELECTION IS HELD OTHER THAN THE ELECTION OF THE PRESIDENT. ACCORDINGLY, AN INJUNCTION IS ISSUED AGAINST THE WONG FAMILY ASSOCIATION PRECLUDING THEM FROM CONDUCTING ANY OTHER ELECTION, WHETHER FOR DIRECTORS OF THE GHEE GONG GROUP OR OTHERWISE. PLAINTIFFS STANLEY WONG, MOON WONG AND FUNG SUN WONG SHALL POST AN UNDERTAKING IN THE AMOUNT OF $20,000.00 WHICH MUST BE ISSUED NO LATER THAN SATURDAY, DECEMBER 13, 2003 AT 4 PM AND BE FILED WITH THE SUPERIOR COURT ON 12/15/03. THE ELECTION OF PRESIDENT WILL BE HELD AT 25 ANG HONG PLACE, SAN FRANCISCO; ANY MEMBER WHO IS QUALIFIED TO VOTE, MAY VOTE (ONE VOTE, ONE PERSON). THE COURT MAKES NO FINDING ON ANY ISSUE RELATED TO THE ELECTION OF ANY DIRECTORS OF ANY GROUPS WITHIN THE WONG FAMILY ASSOCIATION. PER DIEM FEES ARE ASSESSED PURSUANT TO GC 72711 AND 72711.5 JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: JUNE LEATHLEY CSR 1629
BEFORE HEARING, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR BY COURT. (212)

ADDED TO LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR Ex Parte Hearing Re Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-12-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Donald Mah, Esq., Howard, Lytle, Petersen & Mah (650) 992-3200 For Plaintiffs Sik Hoi Wong, Stanley Wong, Moon T. Wong and Fung Sun Wong
David Yang, Esq. (415) 982-7315 for Defendants David Wong and Wong Family Association
RULING:
EX PARTE HEARING RE: INJUNCTION
The Court finds: that the integrity of the 12/14/03 presidential election ordered by the Court on 11/14/03 by stipulation of the parties will be endangered if any election is held other than the election of the president. Accordingly, an injunction is issued against the Wong Family Association precluding them from conducting any other election, whether for directors of the Ghee Gong group or otherwise.
Plaintiffs Stanley Wong, Moon Wong and Fung Sun Wong shall post an undertaking in the amount of $20,000.00 which must be issued no later than Saturday, December 13, 2003 at 4 PM and be filed with the Superior Court on 12/15/03.
The election of president will be held at 25 Ang Hong Place, San Francisco; any member who is qualified to vote, may vote (one vote, one person).
The Court makes no finding on any issue related to the election of any directors of any groups within the Wong Family Association.
Per diem fees are assessed pursuant to GC 72711 and 72711.5 (301/JLW/BH) Reporter: June Leathley CSR 1629
